verses.latin_vulgate: Non turbetur cor vestrum. Creditis in Deum, et in me credite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ταρασσέσθω ὑμῶν ἡ καρδία πιστεύετε εἰς τὸν θεόν καὶ εἰς ἐμὲ πιστεύετε
verses.king_james: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: In domo Patris mei mansiones multæ sunt; si quominus dixissem vobis: quia vado parare vobis locum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ τοῦ πατρός μου μοναὶ πολλαί εἰσιν εἰ δὲ μή εἶπον ἂν ὑμῖν πορεύομαι ἑτοιμάσαι τόπον ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: In my Father's house are many mansions: if *it were* not *so*, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si abiero, et præparavero vobis locum, iterum venio, et accipiam vos ad meipsum: ut ubi sum ego, et vos sitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν πορευθῶ καὶ ἑτοιμάσω ὑμῖν τόπον πάλιν ἔρχομαι καὶ παραλήψομαι ὑμᾶς πρὸς ἐμαυτόν ἵνα ὅπου εἰμὶ ἐγὼ καὶ ὑμεῖς ἦτε
verses.king_james: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, *there* ye may be also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quo ego vado scitis, et viam scitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅπου ἐγὼ ὑπάγω οἴδατε καὶ τὴν ὁδόν οἴδατε,
verses.king_james: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Thomas: Domine, nescimus quo vadis: et quomodo possumus viam scire?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ θωμᾶς κύριε οὐκ οἴδαμεν ποῦ ὑπάγεις καὶ πῶς δυνάμεθα τὴν ὁδὸν εἰδέναι
verses.king_james: Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Ego sum via, et veritas, et vita. Nemo venit ad Patrem, nisi per me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ὁδὸς καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια καὶ ἡ ζωή οὐδεὶς ἔρχεται πρὸς τὸν πατέρα εἰ μὴ δι᾽ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si cognovissetis me, et Patrem meum utique cognovissetis: et amodo cognoscetis eum, et vidistis eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ἐγνώκειτέ με καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου ἐγνώκειτε ἂν καὶ ἀπ᾽ ἄρτι γινώσκετε αὐτὸν καὶ ἑωράκατε αὐτόν
verses.king_james: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Philippus: Domine, ostende nobis Patrem, et sufficit nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ φίλιππος κύριε δεῖξον ἡμῖν τὸν πατέρα καὶ ἀρκεῖ ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Tanto tempore vobiscum sum, et non cognovistis me? Philippe, qui videt me, videt et Patrem. Quomodo tu dicis: Ostende nobis Patrem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς τοσοῦτον χρόνον μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν εἰμι καὶ οὐκ ἔγνωκάς με φίλιππε ὁ ἑωρακὼς ἐμὲ ἑώρακεν τὸν πατέρα καὶ πῶς σὺ λέγεις δεῖξον ἡμῖν τὸν πατέρα
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou *then*, Shew us the Father?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non creditis quia ego in Patre, et Pater in me est? Verba quæ ego loquor vobis, a meipso non loquor. Pater autem in me manens, ipse fecit opera.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ πιστεύεις ὅτι ἐγὼ ἐν τῷ πατρὶ καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ἐν ἐμοί ἐστιν τὰ ῥήματα ἃ ἐγὼ λαλῶ ὑμῖν ἀπ᾽ ἐμαυτοῦ οὐ λαλῶ, ὁ δὲ πατὴρ ὁ ἐν ἐμοὶ μένων αὐτὸς ποιεῖ τὰ ἔργα
verses.king_james: Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non creditis quia ego in Patre, et Pater in me est?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστεύετέ μοι ὅτι ἐγὼ ἐν τῷ πατρὶ καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ἐν ἐμοί εἰ δὲ μή διὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτὰ πιστεύετε μοι
verses.king_james: Believe me that I *am* in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works' sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: alioquin propter opera ipsa credite. Amen, amen dico vobis, qui credit in me, opera quæ ego facio, et ipse faciet, et majora horum faciet: quia ego ad Patrem vado.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ τὰ ἔργα ἃ ἐγὼ ποιῶ κἀκεῖνος ποιήσει καὶ μείζονα τούτων ποιήσει ὅτι ἐγὼ πρὸς τὸν πατέρα μου πορεύομαι
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater *works* than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quodcumque petieritis Patrem in nomine meo, hoc faciam: ut glorificetur Pater in Filio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅ τι ἂν αἰτήσητε ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου τοῦτο ποιήσω ἵνα δοξασθῇ ὁ πατὴρ ἐν τῷ υἱῷ
verses.king_james: And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quid petieritis me in nomine meo, hoc faciam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐάν τι αἰτήσητέ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου ἐγὼ ποιήσω
verses.king_james: If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si diligitis me, mandata mea servate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν ἀγαπᾶτέ με τὰς ἐντολὰς τὰς ἐμὰς τηρήσατε
verses.king_james: If ye love me, keep my commandments.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego rogabo Patrem, et alium Paraclitum dabit vobis, ut maneat vobiscum in æternum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγὼ ἐρωτήσω τὸν πατέρα καὶ ἄλλον παράκλητον δώσει ὑμῖν ἵνα μένῃ μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα
verses.king_james: And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritum veritatis, quem mundus non potest accipere, quia non videt eum, nec scit eum: vos autem cognoscetis eum, quia apud vos manebit, et in vobis erit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ πνεῦμα τῆς ἀληθείας ὃ ὁ κόσμος οὐ δύναται λαβεῖν ὅτι οὐ θεωρεῖ αὐτὸ οὐδὲ γινώσκει αὐτό ὑμεῖς δὲ γινώσκετε αὐτὸ, ὅτι παρ᾽ ὑμῖν μένει καὶ ἐν ὑμῖν ἔσται
verses.king_james: *Even* the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non relinquam vos orphanos: veniam ad vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἀφήσω ὑμᾶς ὀρφανούς ἔρχομαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc modicum, et mundus me jam non videt. Vos autem videtis me: quia ego vivo, et vos vivetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔτι μικρὸν καὶ ὁ κόσμος με οὐκ ἔτι θεωρεῖ ὑμεῖς δὲ θεωρεῖτέ με ὅτι ἐγὼ ζῶ καὶ ὑμεῖς ζήσεσθε
verses.king_james: Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.

verses.latin_vulgate: In illo die vos cognoscetis quia ego sum in Patre meo, et vos in me, et ego in vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ γνώσεσθε ὑμεῖς ὅτι ἐγὼ ἐν τῷ πατρί μου καὶ ὑμεῖς ἐν ἐμοὶ κἀγὼ ἐν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: At that day ye shall know that I *am* in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui habet mandata mea, et servat ea: ille est qui diligit me. Qui autem diligit me, diligetur a Patre meo: et ego diligam eum, et manifestabo ei meipsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἔχων τὰς ἐντολάς μου καὶ τηρῶν αὐτὰς ἐκεῖνός ἐστιν ὁ ἀγαπῶν με ὁ δὲ ἀγαπῶν με ἀγαπηθήσεται ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρός μου καὶ ἐγὼ ἀγαπήσω αὐτὸν καὶ ἐμφανίσω αὐτῷ ἐμαυτόν
verses.king_james: He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Judas, non ille Iscariotes: Domine, quid factum est, quia manifestaturus es nobis teipsum, et non mundo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ἰούδας οὐχ ὁ ἰσκαριώτης κύριε τί γέγονεν ὅτι ἡμῖν μέλλεις ἐμφανίζειν σεαυτὸν καὶ οὐχὶ τῷ κόσμῳ
verses.king_james: Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Si quis diligit me, sermonem meum servabit, et Pater meus diliget eum, et ad eum veniemus, et mansionem apud eum faciemus;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἐάν τις ἀγαπᾷ με τὸν λόγον μου τηρήσει καὶ ὁ πατήρ μου ἀγαπήσει αὐτόν καὶ πρὸς αὐτὸν ἐλευσόμεθα καὶ μονὴν παρ᾽ αὐτῷ ποιησόμεν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui non diligit me, sermones meos non servat. Et sermonem, quem audistis, non est meus: sed ejus qui misit me, Patris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μὴ ἀγαπῶν με τοὺς λόγους μου οὐ τηρεῖ καὶ ὁ λόγος ὃν ἀκούετε οὐκ ἔστιν ἐμὸς ἀλλὰ τοῦ πέμψαντός με πατρός
verses.king_james: He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father's which sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc locutus sum vobis apud vos manens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν παρ᾽ ὑμῖν μένων
verses.king_james: These things have I spoken unto you, being *yet* present with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Paraclitus autem Spiritus Sanctus, quem mittet Pater in nomine meo, ille vos docebit omnia, et suggeret vobis omnia quæcumque dixero vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ παράκλητος τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ὃ πέμψει ὁ πατὴρ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου ἐκεῖνος ὑμᾶς διδάξει πάντα καὶ ὑπομνήσει ὑμᾶς πάντα ἃ εἶπον ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: But the Comforter, *which is* the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pacem relinquo vobis, pacem meam do vobis: non quomodo mundus dat, ego do vobis. Non turbetur cor vestrum, neque formidet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰρήνην ἀφίημι ὑμῖν εἰρήνην τὴν ἐμὴν δίδωμι ὑμῖν οὐ καθὼς ὁ κόσμος δίδωσιν ἐγὼ δίδωμι ὑμῖν μὴ ταρασσέσθω ὑμῶν ἡ καρδία μηδὲ δειλιάτω
verses.king_james: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audistis quia ego dixi vobis: Vado, et venio ad vos. Si diligeretis me, gauderetis utique, quia vado ad Patrem: quia Pater major me est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἐγὼ εἶπον ὑμῖν ὑπάγω καὶ ἔρχομαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς εἰ ἠγαπᾶτέ με ἐχάρητε ἄν ὅτι εἶπον πορεύομαι πρὸς τὸν πατέρα ὅτι ὁ πατὴρ μού μείζων μού ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come *again* unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc dixi vobis priusquam fiat: ut cum factum fuerit, credatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ νῦν εἴρηκα ὑμῖν πρὶν γενέσθαι ἵνα ὅταν γένηται πιστεύσητε
verses.king_james: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam non multa loquar vobiscum: venit enim princeps mundi hujus, et in me non habet quidquam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔτι πολλὰ λαλήσω μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν ἔρχεται γὰρ ὁ τοῦ κόσμου τούτου ἄρχων καὶ ἐν ἐμοὶ οὐκ ἔχει οὐδέν
verses.king_james: Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ut cognoscat mundus quia diligo Patrem, et sicut mandatum dedit mihi Pater, sic facio. Surgite, eamus hinc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα γνῷ ὁ κόσμος ὅτι ἀγαπῶ τὸν πατέρα καὶ καθὼς ἐνετείλατο μοι ὁ πατήρ οὕτως ποιῶ ἐγείρεσθε ἄγωμεν ἐντεῦθεν
verses.king_james: But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.
